[High] Empty diff means nothing is approvable as a code change (architect, security, data_correctness).
[High] Missing auth/CSRF protections for localhost mutation endpoints (security; architect supports with local-loopback auth concern).
[High] Path traversal risk in planned /reviews/:id filesystem reads (security).
[High] Stored XSS risk when rendering synthesis.md/review artifacts (security).
[High] Browser bootstrap contract is broken/unclear (daemon.lock/home-file discovery in web runtime) (architect, maintainability).
[High] Client-side-only filtering/pagination for large datasets will not scale (performance).
[High] Large-list rendering without virtualization risks UI collapse (performance).
[High] dispatch-events represented as SSE when route is JSON-only (spec mismatch) (maintainability).
[High] External unreachable architectural dependency (llamactl/packages/app) in core spec guidance (maintainability).
[High] Upfront complexity/premise risk: pixel-perfect custom UI and heavy scaffolding for internal tool (devils_advocate, simplicity).
[Medium] Global always-on SSE subscriptions over-couple modules and increase load (architect, performance).
[Medium] API contract/versioning not explicit; backend surface may drift during phased rollout (architect, devils_advocate, maintainability).
[Medium] Daemon URL fallback can allow remote redirection/SSRF-like exfil if not loopback-restricted (security).
[Medium] Missing test strategy for console package (stores/hooks/SSE/UI contracts) (maintainability).
[Medium] Error/loading/empty/downtime behavior deferred or undefined for early live phases (maintainability).
[Medium] Spec over-scopes early phases (too many routes/stubs/stores/themes/palette before core loop is proven) (simplicity).
[Medium] Tauri phase introduces major supply-chain/ops risk if updater trust/signing policy is not mandatory (security, devils_advocate).
[Low] Naming clarity issues (disp/res, ui-store, GH, etc.) reduce ergonomics but are non-blocking (naming_clarity).
[High] Build-time token injection leaks bearer secret to browser bundle (security).
[High] SSE CRLF framing is not parsed, causing silent event loss (data_correctness).
[High] Stream can stall permanently after clean EOF because reconnect path is not triggered (data_correctness).
[High] Reconnect backoff counter never resets after successful reconnect, causing pathological delays (maintainability).
[High] Unbounded event retention/update strategy in useStream causes memory growth and O(n) update cost (architect), with related parser-side unbounded buffering risk (security).
[Medium] Remote override mode can exfiltrate bearer token to arbitrary host (security).
[Medium] Stream transport is coupled directly to global UI store, weakening layering/reuse (architect).
[Medium] Validated responses are returned as Promise<unknown>, discarding type contracts (architect, naming_clarity, simplicity).
[Medium] Dead/duplicated styling surface (duplicate token files, large unimplemented CSS blocks, commented-out themes) (simplicity, maintainability).
[Medium] Hardcoded version duplicated in multiple components/tests, drift risk (maintainability).
[Medium] Loopback allowlist excludes IPv6 ([::1]) and is brittle as regex gate (maintainability).
[Medium] Stream message shape uses fake sentinel in raw field (MessageEvent | {reconnect:true}), weakening type correctness (naming_clarity).
[Low] Naming/ergonomics debt (vague public names, repetitive magic strings, terse CSS class names, opaque error text) (naming_clarity).
[Low] Minor YAGNI/abstraction noise (tiny wrapper components, unused test injection hook, verbose option shaping) (simplicity).
[High] Browser-exposed daemon bearer token (bundle/meta/config path) can leak privileged auth (security, devils_advocate).
[High] Stream transport is tightly coupled to global UI store and process-wide singleton broker (multi-context scaling/testability risk) (architect).
[High] SSE transport robustness gaps: CRLF-framed events can be dropped and parser permits unbounded buffer growth (DoS vector) (data_correctness, security).

[Medium] Stream topic naming/API shape is inconsistent (-events vs -stream) and forces special-case mapping (naming_clarity).
[Medium] Module registry/navigation IDs are duplicated across router/rail/store (drift risk) (architect).

[High] Contract drift: config accepts hosted_mcp_servers before runtime owns/uses it.
[Medium] Duplicate MCP contract definitions risk divergence.
[Medium] Whitespace-only command passes validation but is non-executable.
[Medium] hosted_mcp_servers uses .optional() instead of normalized empty-list semantics.
[Medium] env allows duplicate variable names with undefined resolution semantics.
[Medium] Hosted MCP schema lacks lifecycle/intent documentation.

Same-name MCP server reuse ignores spec drift (command/args/env), enabling stale runtime behavior and cross-agent server confusion. Personas: architect, data_correctness, security.
Hosted MCP subprocesses inherit full daemon environment, exposing ambient secrets to compromised/malicious child servers. Personas: security.
MCP transport contract is hard-coded to newline-delimited JSON framing, risking incompatibility with compliant MCP servers and hidden lock-in. Personas: architect.
Reactor shutdown does not await async pool shutdown (void call), so child-process teardown completion is not guaranteed. Personas: maintainability, architect.
Reload is non-atomic/fail-fast: state clears occur before fallible MCP bootstrap, so one failure can abort reconciliation and leave partial scheduler state. Personas: architect, data_correctness.
Unbounded stdout buffer accumulation allows subprocess-driven memory DoS (no max line/buffer limit). Personas: security.
callTool duplicates JSON-RPC send/timeout/pending logic instead of reusing helper, increasing bug-fix divergence risk. Personas: simplicity, maintainability.
Services.mcpPool type is overly narrowed (shutdown only), potentially blocking legitimate future consumers through the service bag. Personas: maintainability (with weaker pushback from simplicity).
Migration warning may log sensitive legacy MCP config values; redact to metadata-only logs. Personas: security.
Test gap for callTool unknown-server branch (mcp_unavailable). Personas: maintainability.
Magic interval constants / configurability/documentation gaps (DEFAULT_GRACE_MS, sweep interval). Personas: maintainability.

Minor cleanup: no-op states.set in teardown, one-off helper over-abstraction (envFromSpec), dual logger path ambiguity, fragile any cast for mcpServers, race-prone sleep in test. Personas: simplicity,
New endpoint is not wired to a runtime ActionGate, so it can degrade to action_gate_not_configured in real runtime paths. Personas: architect.
Tool-name regex rejects valid dotted long-lived tool IDs, causing false invalid_input before policy evaluation. Personas: architect, data_correctness.

Route re-authenticates with PENUMBRA_TOKEN env and plain equality, conflicting with centralized daemon auth/token source and causing pass-global/fail-local behavior. Personas: architect, simplicity, d
Caller-controlled (agent_id, tick_id) allows cross-agent impersonation for any bearer-token holder; identity is not caller-bound. Personas: security.
Open-tick authorization is replayable (no nonce/sequence/signed freshness binding). Personas: security.
TOCTOU race: tick may close after identity check but before handleCall, allowing stale-identity execution. Personas: data_correctness.
Local json() helper duplicates shared route helper. Personas: simplicity.
identity.ts abstraction has one call site and may be unnecessary indirection (non-blocking cleanup candidate). Personas: simplicity.
ActionGateLike includes currently unused fields in this path (pending_action_id, tier, snapshot_id), potential YAGNI cleanup. Personas: simplicity.
No explicit body/args size/depth guard; authenticated oversized payloads can cause resource pressure. Personas: security.
Queued response includes hardcoded instructional message likely to drift. Personas: simplicity.
Repetitive test setup (large repeated agent insert; manual Services stubs) increases drift/noise. Personas: simplicity.

services.mcpPool dependency is implicit/under-typed, increasing runtime drift risk (maintainability).
buildServer async conversion adds cross-file churn; tradeoff unresolved (simplicity).
Duplicated error-shaping branches in federation client increase maintenance burden (simplicity).
mcpCall wrapper is single-consumer abstraction (possible YAGNI) (simplicity).
Queued instruction string duplicates daemon-provided message contract (simplicity).

services.log.warn without guard/assert can crash if logger wiring regresses (maintainability).
Unused optional agentId/tickId options add cognitive overhead (maintainability).

Single-use local abstractions (FederationToolDescriptor, tiny parse helper) may be over-factored unless reused (simplicity).

[H1] Client-forgeable verifier provenance (integrity/authz risk). Caller-provided by/session_id is written as authoritative provenance without identity binding. Raised by: security (primary), architec
[H2] “Audit” is last-write-wins, not historical. Verification context is overwritten in t2_memories; prior verification events are lost. Raised by: architect, devils_advocate.

[H3] Recall contract inconsistency drops provenance on hydration. search returns provenance but getObservations/ObservationDetailSchema do not, breaking the documented search→timeline→get_observations

[M4] Provenance fields are unbounded/underspecified free text. No length/format/vocabulary constraints; enables inconsistent values and possible storage/response amplification. Raised by: security, ar
[M5] Missing route/tool integration tests for new params. Optional fields added at daemon/MCP boundaries lack forwarding/mapping tests. Raised by: maintainability.
[M6] Shared SearchHit shape is accumulating tier-specific fields. Flat schema now carries t2-only verification metadata, increasing contract ambiguity. Raised by: architect.
[M7] Consumer gap / potential YAGNI surface bloat. New columns/fields/routes may be ahead of active production consumers. Raised by: simplicity (disputed by maintainability’s contract-gap finding).
[L8] Naming clarity issues in public/API and opts fields. by/reason are vague; sessionId vs session_id mismatch adds translation friction. Raised by: naming_clarity.
[L9] Implicit NULL lifecycle dependency in writeT2Memory. Insert omits provenance columns and depends on defaults; currently valid but fragile if constraints evolve. Raised by: maintainability.
[I10] No indexes on new provenance columns. No immediate impact now; future filtering would scan. Raised by: performance (informational).

[F1][high] Sweeper scalability/perf regression: full dispatched scan + per-row lookup/filter each tick; DB-side filtering removed. Personas: architect, performance, security, maintainability, devils_a

[F2][high] Task-type threshold tests are miswired: smoke_test/implement_small use default opts and can pass without validating resolver behavior. Personas: maintainability, architect, data_correctness

[F3][high] Scoped typecheck workaround is brittle and expensive: unconditional packages/core prepend plus tsc -b --force may mask dependency-graph issues and increase reconciliation latency. Personas:
[F4][medium] Threshold mapping duplication/drift risk between serve.ts and test helper; no single source of truth. Personas: architect, maintainability, simplicity, naming_clarity.
[F5][medium] packagesToBuild logic duplicated in merge-reconciler command/error paths, increasing maintenance drift risk. Personas: maintainability, simplicity, naming_clarity.
[F6][medium] Option API clarity issue: staleAfterMs name implies scalar but also accepts resolver function; ergonomics/mental-model mismatch. Personas: naming_clarity.
[F7][low] Minor dead/redundant branches (case 'unknown'/case null same as default) and related readability noise. Personas: maintainability, simplicity.
[F8][low] Minor readability/ergonomics nits (toCommandArgs thin wrapper, IIFE normalization, JSDoc ambiguity). Personas: naming_clarity, simplicity, maintainability.
Fix [F1] first: restore DB-side prefiltering (at least coarse ts < now - minTTL), avoid N+1 reads, and add per-tick cap/pagination guardrails.
Fix [F2] immediately: switch those tests to task-aware opts and add boundary assertions (e.g., 14m/16m for implement_small, 4m/6m for smoke_test).
Address [F3] with a bounded mitigation: remove unconditional --force if possible; if temporarily required, gate it narrowly and open a follow-up to validate/fix TS project reference closure.
Refactor for [F4]: extract one typed threshold resolver/map used by daemon wiring and tests.
Clean [F5]-[F8] in a quick follow-up: dedupe packagesToBuild, simplify wrappers/normalization, and remove or document redundant switch branches.
None. All 8 personas returned findings; no dispatch failures reported.
Foreign-key cascade invariant likely not enforced at runtime; stale verification events can survive delete/recreate of the same memory_id.
Provenance contract is semantically unsafe: last_verified_by meaning changed in-place, while claimed_by is client-asserted and spoofable if treated as authenticated identity.
Audit trail boundary is incoherent (write path added, but read contract strategy unclear; exported reader is untested/possibly unused).
last_verified_claimed_by on t2_memories appears to be dead storage (written but not surfaced on core read paths).
Verification event ordering can be wrong on timestamp ties (event_id tie-break is non-temporal); index/order mismatch may require extra sort work.
Writer API coupling: verifyMemory requires infra injection (uuid, clock-style dependency) at call sites, increasing boundary leakage.
Throughput risk: synchronous dual-write transaction in async route can become a contention hotspot under high concurrency.
Verify route response construction is redundant (spread + overrides + dead event_id fallback) and increases cognitive load.
by alias handling is unclear and inconsistent (underdocumented deprecation, regex mismatch with claimed_by, MCP guidance ambiguity).
Reader boundary guard missing: negative limit can disable bounding (LIMIT -1 behavior).
Scope hygiene concern: unrelated reviews_dir hardening bundled with audit/provenance change.

Completion lacks ownership CAS; stale worker can write terminal state after lease loss (completeJob by job_id only, runner continues after failed heartbeat). Personas: security, data_correctness, main
Reclaim race/TOCTOU: stale sweep does SELECT then unconditional UPDATE by job_id, allowing live claims to be clobbered. Personas: architect, security, maintainability.
Layering break: daemon imports packages/core/src/* internals directly, coupling runtime to core file layout/build internals. Personas: architect, maintainability.
sweepStaleJobs takes unused leaseTtlMs (void opts), propagating dead/misleading API surface to callers. Personas: naming_clarity, simplicity, maintainability.
Runner does not abort when heartbeat/lease extension fails; can continue expensive work concurrently with a reclaimed attempt. Personas: maintainability, security.
ts_started is preserved on direct reclaim (COALESCE), corrupting per-attempt timing semantics. Personas: data_correctness.
Sweeper robustness gap: missing interval try/catch + unref, diverging from established sweeper resilience pattern. Personas: architect.
DB efficiency risks: N+1 updates in stale sweep and likely missing indexes for (status, ts_enqueued) / (status, lease_expires_at). Personas: performance.
Shared Services contract absorbs feature-specific mutable liveness state (memoryEfficacyRunnerHeartbeatAt), increasing coupling. Personas: architect.
Enqueue route liveness gate may violate queue decoupling (503 instead of accept-and-queue). Personas: devils_advocate.
Silent external contract drift from pending → queued without migration/deprecation path. Personas: maintainability.
Naming/log/API clarity inconsistencies (sweeper naming/prefixes, generic backend_unavailable, minor variable naming/readability issues). Personas: naming_clarity, simplicity.
[F1 | Medium] Timestamp contract is incorrect/inconsistent and can reject valid ISO timestamps with offsets; validation is also fragmented across layers.
[F2 | Medium] Finding-classification correlation is brittle due to overloaded index and positional coupling (batchIndex + 1 / j + 1) across dual batching loops.
[F3 | Medium] Classification batching is serial and can produce large wall-clock latency on big inputs.
[F4 | Low] Route test setup and assertions are weaker than they should be (partial Services cast, no assertion on validation message quality).
[F5 | Low] Heartbeat staleness threshold is magic-numbered and mirrored in tests (60_000 / 61_000), creating drift risk.
[F6 | Low] Redundant heartbeat reassignment in test is deletable noise.
Embedding-version skew between ingestion and recall indexes causes semantically relevant memories to score below cutoff, returning zero hits on autoRecallForDispatch (architect, data_correctness).
Recall query adds an implicit project_id predicate from stale session cache, excluding the correct project shard even though matching memories exist (data_correctness, maintainability).
Vector compaction job races with dispatch recall and temporarily removes fresh segments from the active ANN graph, producing 0-hit recalls for active topics (performance, architect).
Case-sensitive normalization on concept filters mismatches stored lowercase tags, so eligible memories are filtered out before similarity ranking (data_correctness, simplicity).
Recall path computes freshness cutoff in local time while t2 rows are UTC; boundary memories are incorrectly marked stale and omitted (data_correctness, architect).
Post-migration fallback keeps querying legacy t1-only table when t2 index build status is unknown, hiding valid t2 memories from auto-recall (maintainability, architect).
Recall adapter silently drops non-ASCII tokens during query sanitization, breaking matches for identifiers and names containing accents (data_correctness, maintainability).
Dedup prefilter hashes only summary text and collapses distinct memories with different remediation details, then all survivors fall below topK after collapse (architect, data_correctness).
Read-replica lag on recall queries returns a snapshot before latest memory writes, so dispatch sees zero relevant hits right after observation ingest (performance, data_correctness).
Recall limit is hardcoded to top 3 in one route while scorer expects top 10; strict thresholding on tiny candidate set yields empty final recalls (simplicity, maintainability).
Project alias resolver maps renamed project keys only on write path; recall path still queries old slug and misses all new observations (maintainability, data_correctness).
Memory recall SQL adds `obs_type IN (...)` from an outdated allowlist that excludes promoted t2 types, resulting in no hits despite relevant long-lived memories (architect, maintainability).
ANN index rebuild leaves orphaned namespace metadata for one shard, and recall queries route to an empty namespace for that project (data_correctness, performance).
Staleness comparator treats malformed timestamps as epoch 0 instead of rejecting row, causing broad false-stale filtering in recall candidates (data_correctness, security).
Recall pipeline applies denylist after semantic scoring; if all topK are denied, it does not backfill from lower-ranked hits and returns empty context (architect, maintainability).
AutoRecallForDispatch returns relevant memories, but context builder truncates the recall section at first markdown fence and drops the retrieved guidance (data_correctness, maintainability).
Prompt composer injects recalled items under an optional block disabled in headless mode, so agents never see returned memories during worker dispatch (architect, maintainability).
Recalled memories are present but sorted oldest-first after hydration, burying critical recent guidance beyond model attention budget (performance, data_correctness).
Context formatter strips parenthetical metadata tags globally, accidentally removing the rationale text from recalled findings before dispatch (simplicity, maintainability).
A fallback summarizer rewrites recalled evidence into generic bullets and discards actionable constraints, so the agent proceeds as if memory were absent (architect, data_correctness).
Recall payload is delivered, but dispatcher serializes only `text` and omits `reason`/`severity`, removing the decision signal needed to honor prior findings (maintainability, data_correctness).
Guardrail logic treats recalled memories as low-priority attachments and prunes them when token pressure rises, while less relevant boilerplate is retained (architect, performance).
UI preview shows recalled context, but worker dispatch path uses a separate template without recall injection, creating a false sense of coverage (maintainability, data_correctness).
Hydration returns recalled rows, yet post-hydration dedup keys on normalized title and removes the only memory containing the relevant mitigation step (data_correctness, architect).
Recall is injected into a hidden system segment that one adapter drops during protocol conversion, so downstream model input excludes retrieved memories (architect, maintainability).
Conflict resolver marks recalled and live instructions as incompatible and always prefers live defaults, effectively overriding long-term memory guidance (data_correctness, simplicity).
Agent handoff payload includes `recall_count > 0`, but recalled body is lost during JSON schema narrowing in transport, so worker executes without the retrieved content (architect, data_correctness).
Recalled memories survive dispatch assembly, but response planner ignores the recall section unless a manual flag is present, bypassing relevant context in auto mode (maintainability, architect).
Trace logs confirm recall rows were fetched, but sanitizer redacts key nouns and turns findings into vague placeholders unusable by the model (security, data_correctness).
Merged recall+instruction prompt places memory block after a hard stop delimiter consumed by adapter, causing the model to ignore all retrieved memories (architect, maintainability).
Write path skips observation persistence when classification score is below an undocumented threshold, even for high-severity findings that should register as memory (architect, data_correctness).
Transaction commits handoff metadata before memory.observe insert; rollback on memory write error leaves no long-lived record for future recall (maintainability, data_correctness).
Schema mapper treats empty `reason` as invalid and aborts memory write entirely, discarding findings that still contain actionable text (simplicity, maintainability).
Memory registration is gated behind `isInteractiveSession`; auto chains bypass the gate and never persist observations for later dispatch recall (architect, maintainability).
Bulk ingest worker acknowledges receipt before enqueueing write job; queue backpressure drops jobs silently, so expected memories are never written (performance, data_correctness).
Observation-to-memory promotion cron excludes sessions with custom project tags due to strict enum parse, preventing registration of relevant findings (maintainability, data_correctness).
Dedup gate runs before persistence and rejects new findings sharing checksum with unrelated historical text, causing missed registration of novel failures (architect, data_correctness).
Memory write function returns success on partial field validation failure, but row insert is skipped and no retry is scheduled (maintainability, simplicity).
Shard router sends writes for certain project slugs to a non-existent partition, logging warnings but dropping memory registrations at runtime (architect, data_correctness).
Write-path feature flag defaults off in worker environment; observations are processed but memory registration branch never executes (maintainability, devils_advocate).
Memory.observe adapter drops findings when `severity` is null-mapped from upstream enums, instead of coercing to Unknown and persisting (data_correctness, simplicity).
Retry handler replays failed writes only for network errors; serialization failures are terminal and leave missing memories without dead-letter tracking (architect, maintainability).
Conditional write skips memory creation when agent role is reviewer, even though reviewer findings are intended as long-lived guidance (architect, data_correctness).
Ingest pipeline parses `findingId` as optional and discards rows with transient parse failures instead of retrying, producing silent registration gaps (maintainability, performance).
Memory registration requires provenance fields not populated by one adapter path, so those findings never persist despite successful upstream review completion (architect, maintainability).
